era_context: Late Old Kingdom Egypt (Dynasty VI), Akkadian Empire (Naram-Sin), mature Harappan civilization. This period represents the height of several Bronze Age civilizations, though underlying pressures were building.
disconfirming_evidence: No disconfirming evidence identified. No events are documented for this year.
historiographic_note: The historical record for -2360 is essentially blank. No specific events, inscriptions, or records are known to date to this particular year. The year falls within the reign of Pepi II and Naram-Sin, but no year-specific documentation survives. This reflects the fundamental limitation of ancient historical sources: most years in the third millennium BCE are not documented in any surviving record. Our knowledge of this period comes from general chronological frameworks, archaeological stratigraphy, and occasional inscriptions that do not provide year-by-year detail.
